title: Largest 1-Bordered Square
description:
Given a 2D grid of 0s and 1s, return the number of elements in the largest square subgrid that has all 1s on its border, or 0 if such a subgrid doesn't exist in the grid.

Constraints:
hints: For each square, know how many ones are up, left, down, and right of this square. You can find it in O(N^2) using dynamic programming.
Now for each square ( O(N^3) ), we can evaluate whether that square is 1-bordered in O(1).
